pattern: Memoize the Collatz step count for each integer in [lo, hi], then sort by (steps, value) and return the kth element.
hints: The Collatz sequence always terminates at 1; count steps with recursion or iteration, using a memo table to avoid recomputation.
Build a list of (step_count, value) for all integers in [lo, hi], then sort it—ties in step count break by value ascending.
Return the value at position k-1 of the sorted list (1-indexed k).

edge_inputs.description: example from statement: lo=12, hi=15, k=2
k=1, always the smallest power first
k=hi-lo+1, last element in sorted order
numbers with same power value sorted by integer value
power chain goes high before coming down

stress.description: random lo, hi in [1,1000], k random in valid range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    lo = rng.randint(1, 500)
    hi = rng.randint(lo, 1000)
    k = rng.randint(1, hi - lo + 1)
    return [lo, hi, k]
